prompt: Eye-level architectural shot of a finished Salem NH primary bath remodel. Walls clad in large-format porcelain tile with a soft greige stone-look pattern, vertical 24x48 layout. A freestanding cast-iron tub with a brushed-bronze floor-mount tub filler sits on a heated herringbone porcelain floor. A walk-in glass shower enclosure on the left holds a brushed-bronze rain-head and hand-shower. Floating double vanity in walnut with a single integrated stone trough sink. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, dusk warm tungsten lighting from sconces and a recessed cove, mood is calm and quiet. A small red Anjo-style coffee mug sits on the vanity edge as the only saturated color. High-contrast slightly desaturated palette, NOT HGTV bright, NOT luxury staged. Documentary, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
